shellCommands.command: /bin/bash -lc "pwd && rg --files -g '"'!*node_modules*'"' -g '"'!*.lock'"' | head -200 && printf '\\n--- package files ---\\n' && rg --files -g 'package.json' -g 'next.config.*' -g 'src/**' -g 'app/**' -g 'pages/**' -g 'AGENTS.md' | head -200"

/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' lib/db.js && printf '\\n--- package ---\\n' && sed -n '1,200p' package.json && printf '\\n--- config ---\\n' && sed -n '1,160p' next.config.ts && printf '\\n--- tsconfig ---\\n' && sed -n '1,200p' tsconfig.json"
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,220p' tsconfig.json && printf '\\n--- tag usages ---\\n' && rg -n \"products|revalidateTag|unstable_cache\" app lib --glob '"'!*.css'"'"
/bin/bash -lc 'git diff -- app/page.tsx app/layout.tsx app/globals.css lib/db.js tsconfig.json 2>/dev/null || true'
/bin/bash -lc 'nmem --json m search "product catalog cache products revalidateTag stale while revalidate"'

/bin/bash -lc "nmem --json m add \"The admin product catalog uses one shared Next.js cache tag, products, for all product reads and invalidation. Product reads are wrapped in unstable_cache with a 60-second revalidation window; the page-local Server Action calls revalidateTag(PRODUCTS_CACHE_TAG, 'max') so sync requests return immediately and catalog views converge through stale-while-revalidate behavior.\" -t \"Catalog sync uses shared stale-while-revalidate cache\" --unit-type decision -l catalog -s codex -i 0.8"

errors: `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` is enabled. Enabled hooks may run without review for this invocation.
